project.title: Characterization of Bovine Leukemia Virus Env protein soluble ectodomain expressed in Drosophila S2 cells
project.projectDescription: Shotgun analysis of recombinant  bovine Leukemia Virus Env protein using Drosophila S2 cells as expression system
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Tryptic peptides were analyzed on a nanoLC system (UltiMate 3000, Thermo) coupled to a Q-orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus, Thermo). Peptides were separated on a 75 μm x 50 cm, PepMap TM RSLC C18 column, and eluted using a 90 min linear gradient from 5% to 40% acetonitrile in 0.1% formic acid, at a constant flow rate of 250 nL/min. The mass spectrometer was operated in a data -dependent acquisition mode using a top 12 method.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Protein and peptide identification was performed with PatternLab for Proteomics V using a target-decoy database containing Drosophila melanogaster sequences downloaded from Uniprot (June 2020) to which the sequences of recombinant of bovine Leukemia Virus Env protein and usual proteomics contaminants was added. Searching parameters were set as follows: Peptide mass tolerance 35 ppm, variable modifications on N: Hex3HexNAc2 (+892.3173); dHex Hex(3) HexNAc(2) (+1038.3751); Hex(5)HexNAc(2) (+1216.4229); Hex(6)HexNAc(2) (+1378.4757); Hex(7)HexNAc(2) (+1540.5285) and Hex(8)HexNAc(2) (+1702.5813). Peptide spectrum matches were filtered using a 10-ppm tolerance and FDR ≤1% at the protein level.

labPIs.name: Rosario Durán

references.referenceLine: Tomé-Poderti L, Olivero-Deibe N, Carrión F, Portela MM, Obal G, Cabrera G, Bianchi S, Lima A, Addiego A, Durán R, Moratorio G, Pritsch O. Characterization and application of recombinant Bovine Leukemia Virus Env protein. Sci Rep. 2024 14(1):12190

Comment[Instrument]: Q-orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus, Thermo Scientific)
Comment[Separation]: Peptides were separated on a 75 μm × 50 cm, PepMap RSLC C18 column
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: data -dependent acquisition mode using a top 12 method
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: PNGase F-glycan release followed by 2-aminobenzamide (2AB) labelling and separation of the 2AB derivatives on normal phase HPLC was performed to analyze composition of purified N-glycans in sBLV-EnvFm as follows
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: Collision-induced dissociation MS/MS experiments of selected peptides were performed
